title: UXR Finding
description: A single finding produced by a specialist agent. Every finding MUST be traceable to transcript evidence.

id.description: Stable ID, e.g. PAIN-003, BEH-012, PAPER-005, REC-004, MOM-002, STORY-001.

statement.description: The finding in one clear sentence. No jargon.

method.description: Analysis method used for THIS finding.

method_justification.description: Why this method fits this finding. Required by the hybrid-with-justification rule.

severity.description: 1 = trivial, 5 = blocking. Required for pain_point and papercut.

frequency.description: Number of distinct participants who exhibited/expressed this. MUST NOT exceed the distinct participants in evidence[] ∪ supporting_participants[] (enforced by the frequency_integrity gate). Prefer supporting_participants for the source of truth.

supporting_participants.description: The distinct participant codes that support this finding. Source of truth for frequency; render as n/N in reports.

evidence_mode.description: What KIND of claim this is. Keeps 'a participant said X' separate from 'we observed X' and 'we infer X'.

causal_strength.description: For findings that assert causation. 'none' = descriptive only. Causal statements without behavioral evidence are flagged by causal_support.

alternative_explanations.description: Rival explanations the analyst considered and why this one is preferred. Guards against premature causal claims.

representativeness.description: For themes/moments: is this typical of the sample, a single illustrative case, or a vivid outlier? Prevents a dramatic clip from anchoring a recommendation.

goal_coverage.description: On a synthesis object: per-hypothesis verdict. Each value is { verdict, supporting[], refuting[] }; verdict ∈ supported|refuted|mixed|insufficient_evidence. Refuted/insufficient count as valid coverage.

evidence.description: At least one citation back to a transcript. This is the anti-hallucination anchor.

locator.description: Timestamp (hh:mm:ss) or line reference (L120-L128) inside the transcript.

quote.description: Verbatim excerpt copied from the transcript. Must appear in the source. For 'said' evidence this must be an EXACT verbatim substring (enforced by the quote_exactness gate).

clip_url.description: Marvin video-clip URL for this quote (cut by marvin-clipper). Required for 'said' evidence when config.evidence.requireClips is true.

clip_status.description: Lifecycle of the Marvin clip: pending = queued for marvin-clipper, created = clip_url populated.

observation_type.description: REQUIRED — no silent default. said = participant verbatim (subject to exact-quote/timestamp/clip gates); did = participant action; observed = moderator/observer note.

source_type.description: Provenance of this evidence item. Different source types carry different evidentiary weight.

emotion.description: For powerful_moment: dominant emotion, e.g. frustration, delight, relief, anxiety, pride.

intensity.description: For powerful_moment: emotional intensity / memorability. 5 = unforgettable.

clip_worthy.description: For powerful_moment: true if this is a candidate for a video clip (feeds marvin-clipper).

contradicts.description: IDs of findings this one challenges. Used by Devil's Advocate.
